import Mathlib

open Filter Set

/- A map `T : (α → ℝ≥0) → (α' → ℝ≥0)` is called weakly continuous if for any
  sequence of functions `f_n` in `α → ℝ≥0` that converges pointwise to `f`
  and any bounded set `G` in `α'`, the following holds:
  `eLpNorm (T f) 1 (ν.restrict G) ≤ Filter.limsup (fun n ↦ eLpNorm (T (fs n)) 1 (ν.restrict G)) Filter.atTop`
-/
def WeaklyContinuous [TopologicalSpace ε] [ENorm ε] [ENorm ε'] [SupSet ε] [Preorder ε] (T : (α → ℝ≥0) → (α' → ε')) (p : ℝ≥0∞) (μ : Measure α) (ν : Measure α') : Prop :=
  ∀ {fs : ℕ → SimpleFunc α ℝ≥0} (_ : Monotone fs),
  let f := fun x ↦ ⨆ n, (fs n) x;
  ∀ (_ : MemLorentz f p 1 μ) (G : Set α'),
    eLpNorm (T f) 1 (ν.restrict G) ≤ Filter.limsup (fun n ↦ eLpNorm (T ⇑(fs n)) 1 (ν.restrict G)) Filter.atTop

  let f := fun x ↦ ⨆ n, (fs n) x;
  ∀ (_ : MemLorentz f p 1 μ) (G : Set α'),

    intro f g x
